typing Emerald Theme Manager in terminal nothing happens.
Beryl is working fine also.

please help

### Post by Clay_Banger on 2007-06-11
right click the beryl manager icon in the system tray, then under the menu "select window decorator" make sure that "Standard Beryl Decorator (Emerald) is selected.

---

### Post by LollYouSuckZor on 2007-06-11
> **Clay_Banger said:**
> right click the beryl manager icon in the system tray, then under the menu "select window decorator" make sure that "Standard Beryl Decorator (Emerald) is selected.

He should select "beryl" if it's not already selected.  If that's what you mean.

---

### Post by Clay_Banger on 2007-06-11
> **LollYouSuckZor said:**
> He should select "beryl" if it's not already selected.  If that's what you mean.
No, i mean what i say, the window manager and window decorator are two different things. beryl is the window manager, emerald is the window decorator.

---

### Post by malathia on 2007-06-11
> **Clay_Banger said:**
> No, i mean what i say, the window manager and window decorator are two different things. beryl is the window manager, emerald is the window decorator.

Yeah, this definitely needs to be performed. Beryl will run all of its effects separately from the theme manager installed (emerald, etc.)

In order to utilize the theme functionality, you have to switch from the default theme manager (not on my machine right now, but I think it's listed as just your generic Gnome, KDE, whatever) under beryl-manager>>window decorator

---

### Post by Clay_Banger on 2007-06-12
you could try another window decorator and see how you go with that.
Heliodor and Aquamarine are the other two which beryl can use, and i believe that both are in the repos.

[EDIT]
Taken from wikipedia's artice on beryl:
# Heliodor, another fork of Compiz's gtk-window-decorator, supports Metacity themes.
# Aquamarine supports KWin themes.

You could try them, but it looks like you wont get the looks that emerald will be able to produce
[/EDIT]

---
